prompt: A Bauhaus graphic design poster from 1928, adhering strictly to the visual grammar of Herbert Bayer and László Moholy-Nagy. The poster, titled "DER 'COLD START' PROZESS" (The 'Cold Start' Process), visually maps a recommendation engine trying to categorize a user it has never seen. The composition is dynamic, diagonally organized across an off-white, textured paper stock, with a deep indigo blue, cadmium yellow, and a precise crimson red. At the top, a series of identical, abstract, featureless geometric human silhouettes, rendered in a cold indigo, enter the composition from the right, labeled with dynamic typography as "NEUER NUTZER: KEINE DATEN" (NEW USER: NO DATA). These figures meet a diagonal barrier: a thick crimson red grid intersecting a yellow curve, a schematic map of the recommendation algorithm (labeled "RECOMMENDER-ALGORITHMUS SCHEMATIK"). This intersection represents the data desert. Emerging from the left side of this barrier is a final figure: no longer featureless, but structured. It is composed of contrasting yellow and red circular clusters, interlocking geometric shapes (labeled "REGENERIERTER EMPFEHLUNGS-DATENSATZ" - REGENERATED RECOMMENDATION DATASET), symbolizing the categorized user profile. Small circular and linear patterns and schematic arrows (the machine learning paths) connect these elements, with strict sans-serif (Universal Grotesk) typography: "KEINE PRÄFERENZEN" (NO PREFERENCES), "KEINE HISTORIE" (NO HISTORY), and "SYSTEM-LERNKURVE: STEIL" (SYSTEM LEARNING CURVE: STEEP). The poster has a tactile quality, with visible printer ink density and slight misregistration, all contained within the precise, geometric, functional Bauhaus aesthetic. All text is in authentic 1920s Bauhaus-style typography and orthography.
tradition_approach: Targeted strong diagonal composition, primary color palette, and Universal Grotesk typography. These map the 'cold start' as a functional engineering problem, using geometric reduction to represent the transition from anonymous user to structured data profile.
